info.title: Syndicore API
info.description: Backend API for Syndicore — a cyberpunk real-time strategy MMO. Auth is delegated to Supabase (ES256 JWT via JWKS).

bearerAuth.description: Supabase access token. Verified via JWKS (ES256).

Faction.description: Player faction (per world). Matches Prisma enum.

Ring.description: Map ring — progression order: FRINGE → GRID → CORE → NEXUS.

GameConstants.description: Full contents of shared/game-constants.json. Clients cache by ETag. Authoritative shape is the JSON file itself.

before.description: Cursor — ID of the last item on the previous page

nextCursor.description: Pass as `before` for next page, null if no more

crystal.description: Ring name of the crystal just obtained

newRing.description: Ring the player has advanced into

ruins.description: RuinsSite left on the old city tile

token.description: APNS device token (hex string, typically 64 chars)

deviceId.description: iOS identifierForVendor (UUID) — stable per app install on a device

launchAt.description: ISO 8601 — when the rally window closes and troops depart

get.summary: Get the currently authenticated player + their worlds

404.description: Authenticated but no Player row yet — client must run onboarding

post.summary: Create global Player row (username only — faction chosen when joining a world)

409.description: Player already onboarded OR username taken

get.summary: List available worlds with player counts
get.description: Public — iOS shows this on the server picker screen. No auth required.

post.summary: Join a world — choose faction, get starter city on random Fringe tile

201.description: Joined successfully — returns PlayerWorld + City + tile coordinates

400.description: Invalid faction or player not onboarded yet

409.description: Already joined / world full / world not open

get.description: Returns shared/game-constants.json verbatim. Response includes an ETag; clients should send If-None-Match on subsequent requests to get 304. No authentication required.

ETag.description: Content hash — opaque token for cache validation

get.summary: Paginated list of player troop movements (newest first)

schema.description: Cursor — ID of the last item on the previous page

get.summary: Paginated battle reports for the current player (newest first)

schema.description: Cursor — ID of the last item on the previous page

200.description: Page of battle reports with cursor

post.summary: Implode a maxed city and instantly re-found in the next ring
post.description: Body is empty — BE picks the target tile (ruins in the next ring with no incoming movements first, falling back to a random empty tile). Requires HQ 20, a trained Settler, no active construction, and zero troop movements originating from or targeting this city.

200.description: Implosion successful — crystal minted, new city founded

post.summary: Recall your reinforcements from another player's city
post.description: Deletes the reinforcement record and creates a RETURN movement back to the caller's current city. Atomic.

200.description: Recall scheduled — troops are on their way home

post.summary: Register an APNS device token for push notifications

delete.summary: Unregister an APNS device token on logout
delete.description: Scoped to the caller — only their own token is deleted.

post.description: Caller becomes the rally creator and participant #1; their committed troops are deducted from their city immediately. Same-syndikat members can join via /:id/join until launchAt. Requires a built Rally Point (level unlocks max concurrent rallies per game-constants).

400.description: invalid_input / invalid_launch_at / launch_at_in_past / invalid_target_tile / invalid_units / unknown_unit / no_units_selected

409.description: not_in_syndikat / no_city / rally_point_required / rally_point_level_too_low / max_active_rallies_reached / insufficient_troops

get.summary: Active rallies (FORMING + LAUNCHED) for the caller's syndikat

200.description: Rally list (empty array if caller has no syndikat)

post.summary: Join an active rally (same-syndikat members only)
post.description: Commits the caller's troops to the rally. Their troops are deducted from their city right away. Calling again merges into the existing row.

post.summary: Participant pulls out of a rally before launch
post.description: Troops are refunded to the participant's city. The creator cannot leave — they must cancel.

delete.summary: Creator cancels the whole rally before launch
delete.description: All participants get their troops refunded.

post.summary: Claim this device as the active session — hard-kicks any other device
post.description: Single-active-session enforcement. After login, iOS sends its identifierForVendor UUID. If a different device was the active one, it receives a Socket.IO `session_kicked` event and its subsequent API calls fail with 401 `session_invalidated`. Same-device re-claim (app relaunch with the same IDFV) is silent. Requires an existing Player row — run /me/onboarding first.

delete.description: Idempotent. After this, the device must re-claim to make any authenticated API call.
